name: Drum Island
description: Drum Island is a perpetual winter island in Paradise, known as the birthplace of Dr. Hiriluk's dream and home to Tony Tony Chopper. Once ruled by the tyrannical King Wapol, the island was liberated by the Straw Hats and transformed into the peaceful Sakura Kingdom under Dr. Kureha and King Dalton. The island's cherry blossoms bloom year-round thanks to Hiriluk's dream becoming reality.
history: Drum Kingdom was once renowned as the world's most advanced medical nation, home to the legendary 'Isshi-20' - twenty of the world's finest doctors. This golden age ended when the tyrannical Wapol became king and exiled all doctors except his personal 'Isshi-20' to maintain absolute control. When the pirate Blackbeard attacked, Wapol fled like a coward, abandoning his people. Dr. Hiriluk, a quack doctor with a noble dream, died trying to cure the country's 'frozen heart.' His adopted son, the reindeer Chopper, was mentored by the 139-year-old Dr. Kureha. When Wapol returned to reclaim his throne, Luffy defeated him atop Drum Castle. Dalton became the new king, and Hiriluk's dream of cherry blossoms curing the nation's heart was realized through Dr. Kureha's 'Hiriluk Sakura' - pink snow that falls year-round, transforming Drum Kingdom into the Sakura Kingdom.
culture: Drum Island's culture centers on survival in extreme cold and reverence for medical knowledge. The people are hardy, resilient, and deeply scarred by Wapol's tyranny that weaponized healthcare. Before Wapol, doctors were celebrated as saviors; under his rule, medical care became a privilege for the wealthy. After liberation, the culture shifted toward Hiriluk's philosophy: that a country can be saved not by doctors, but by people with strong, caring hearts. The year-round 'sakura snow' symbolizes hope, healing, and renewal. Citizens now celebrate the Pirate Flag Festival honoring the Straw Hats, and Chopper is remembered as a national hero.
transportation: Most travel is by foot or sled through deep snow. Climbing equipment is essential for mountain traversal. Lapahns (aggressive snow rabbits) and Hiking Bears roam the wilderness. Dr. Kureha famously travels via sled pulled by reindeer or her own strength. Ships dock at coastal ports but must contend with freezing waters and icebergs. The ropeway system connects lower villages to Drum Castle at the peak of the Drum Rockies.

traditions: Celebrating with feasts after successful hunts
Sharing food with travelers (hospitality is survival)
quirks: Dr. Kureha drinks heavily but never gets sick
Villagers stockpile food for harsh winters

notablePeople.name: Tony Tony Chopper
notablePeople.description: A blue-nosed reindeer who ate the Human-Human Fruit, granting him human intelligence and transformations. Trained by Dr. Kureha and inspired by Dr. Hiriluk's dream. Joined the Straw Hats as their doctor after Luffy defeated Wapol.

notablePeople.name: Dr. Kureha
notablePeople.description: The 139-year-old 'Witch' and greatest doctor on Drum Island. Mentored Chopper and preserved Hiriluk's will. Known for her eccentric personality, incredible medical skill, and her secret to eternal youth.

notablePeople.name: Dr. Hiriluk
notablePeople.description: A terminally ill quack doctor who believed miracles could cure any disease. Adopted Chopper and taught him that a doctor's duty is to heal hearts, not just bodies. Died heroically trying to cure the country with his exploding 'cure.'

notablePeople.name: Wapol
notablePeople.description: The selfish, gluttonous former king who ate the Munch-Munch Fruit. Exiled all doctors and monopolized healthcare. Fled when Blackbeard attacked, then returned to reclaim his throne. Defeated by Luffy and became a pirate.

notablePeople.name: Dalton
notablePeople.description: The noble former captain of Wapol's guard who opposed the king's cruelty. Tried to stop Wapol's return and was nearly killed. After Wapol's defeat, became the beloved king of the renamed Sakura Kingdom.

notablePeople.name: Chess
notablePeople.description: Wapol's scheming minister who resembles a chess piece. Uses bow and arrow attacks. Fiercely loyal to Wapol despite his cowardice.

notablePeople.name: Kuromarimo
notablePeople.description: Wapol's afro-wearing minister who uses his spherical hair to store weapons. Fights with martial arts and hidden weaponry. Partner to Chess.

notablePeople.name: Chessmarimo
notablePeople.description: The fusion of Chess and Kuromarimo created by Wapol's Munch-Munch powers. Combines their abilities into one warrior. Defeated by Chopper's bravery.

notablePeople.name: Robson
notablePeople.description: An elderly guard of Bighorn village who helped the Straw Hats. Represents the common people who suffered under Wapol's rule.

notablePeople.name: Isshi-20
notablePeople.description: Twenty of the world's finest doctors who served Drum Kingdom. Wapol exiled all but his personal physicians to monopolize medical care. Their exile ended the medical golden age.

notablePeople.name: Marshall D. Teach (Blackbeard)
notablePeople.description: The pirate who attacked Drum Kingdom before the main story, causing Wapol to flee. This attack was Blackbeard's early appearance in the series.

subLocations.name: Drum Castle
subLocations.description: The massive castle atop the Drum Rockies at 5,000 meters elevation. Once Wapol's palace, now Dr. Kureha's medical facility and home. The site of Luffy's battle with Wapol and where Hiriluk's Pirate Flag flies eternally.
notableFeatures: Dr. Hiriluk's Pirate Flag on the highest point
Dr. Kureha's medical library and research lab
Year-round view of the entire island

subLocations.name: Drum Rockies
subLocations.description: The treacherous mountain range that dominates the island, covered in perpetual snow and ice. Home to dangerous wildlife including Lapahns and Hiking Bears. The climb is deadly without proper equipment or guidance.

subLocations.name: Bighorn Village
subLocations.description: The main village at the base of the Drum Rockies where the Straw Hats first arrived. A humble settlement that suffered greatly under Wapol's medical monopoly.

subLocations.name: Cocoa Weed Village
subLocations.description: Another village on Drum Island that suffered under Wapol's rule. Dalton served here and protected the citizens.

subLocations.name: Gyasta Village
subLocations.description: A coastal village where ships can dock. The Merry Go was anchored here while the crew climbed the mountain.
notableFeatures: Where Sanji and Luffy began their climb

subLocations.name: Hiriluk's Grave
subLocations.description: The peaceful grave site where Dr. Hiriluk is buried, marked by his Jolly Roger flag. A place of pilgrimage for those who knew him.

items.name: Dr. Hiriluk's Pirate Flag
items.description: The skull-and-crossbones with cherry blossoms that represents Hiriluk's dream. Now flies atop Drum Castle, visible from the entire island. Symbolizes that a country is healthy when people can laugh and smile.

items.name: Rumble Ball
items.description: Chopper's invention that allows him to access additional transformation points beyond normal Zoan limits. Can be dangerous if overused (three within six hours causes Monster Point).

items.name: Hiriluk's Cure (Skull Bomb)
items.description: Hiriluk's final creation - a 'cure' made from a skull-shaped fungus that was actually a powerful explosive. He drank it himself in his final heroic act.

items.name: Dr. Kureha's Plum Wine
items.description: Dr. Kureha's special medicinal plum wine. Her 'secret to youth' that she charges 498 berries (plus 2 for consultation fee) to reveal: 'I'm beautiful because I'm me!'

items.name: Wapol's Crown
items.description: The golden crown of Drum Kingdom's monarchy. Lost when Luffy defeated Wapol and sent him flying off the island.

items.name: Medical Encyclopedia
items.description: The comprehensive medical texts in Drum Castle's library that represent centuries of medical knowledge. Chopper studied these to become a great doctor.

items.name: Cherry Blossom Dust
items.description: The pink powder Dr. Kureha fires into the sky creating artificial cherry blossom snow. Hiriluk's dream made real - the miracle that heals hearts.

items.name: Wapol Metal (Wapomet)
items.description: A unique shape-memory alloy created by Wapol's Munch-Munch powers. Later becomes valuable, making him a wealthy toy manufacturer.

factions.name: Sakura Kingdom Citizens
factions.description: The resilient people of Drum Island who endured Wapol's tyranny and now thrive under Dalton's just rule. They honor the Straw Hats as liberators.

factions.name: Wapol Pirates
factions.description: The remnants of Wapol's forces who returned to reclaim the kingdom. Defeated and scattered by the Straw Hats.

factions.name: Drum Kingdom Royal Guard (Former)
factions.description: The former royal guards who served Wapol reluctantly. Many, like Dalton, opposed his cruelty. Now serve the Sakura Kingdom loyally.

factions.name: Medical Community
factions.description: The doctors who survived Wapol's purge or returned after his defeat. Working to restore Drum Island's reputation as a medical paradise.

factions.name: Blackbeard Pirates (Invaders)
factions.description: The crew that attacked Drum Kingdom, causing Wapol to flee. Their attack inadvertently freed the island from tyranny.

events: Straw Hats arrive seeking a doctor for sick Nami
Learn about Wapol's tyranny and the lack of doctors
Wapol returns to reclaim his throne
Dalton fights Wapol and is nearly killed
Luffy and Sanji climb the Drum Rockies carrying Nami
Meet Dr. Kureha and Chopper at Drum Castle
Dr. Hiriluk's death and final words shown
Luffy protects Hiriluk's flag from Wapol
Battle: Sanji vs Chess and Kuromarimo
Battle: Chopper vs Chessmarimo (proves his courage)
Battle: Luffy vs Wapol (Gomu Gomu no Bazooka victory)
Dr. Kureha fires cherry blossom dust into the sky
Hiriluk's dream realized - cherry blossoms bloom
Chopper joins the Straw Hat crew
Dalton becomes king of the Sakura Kingdom
Straw Hats depart with new doctor and friend

quickFacts: Perpetual winter island with year-round snow
Home to Dr. Kureha, the 139-year-old witch
Once the world's greatest medical nation
Cherry blossoms fall year-round after liberation
Renamed from Drum Kingdom to Sakura Kingdom
Site of Dr. Hiriluk's legendary sacrifice
Wapol exiled all doctors to monopolize healthcare
Attacked by Blackbeard before the Straw Hats arrived
Drum Castle sits at 5,000 meters elevation
Hiriluk's Pirate Flag flies from the highest point

videos.title: Dr. Hiriluk's Death - When Does A Man Die?
videos.description: The legendary scene where Hiriluk sacrifices himself and teaches that a man dies when he is forgotten. One of the most emotional moments in One Piece.

videos.title: Luffy vs Wapol - Protecting Hiriluk's Flag
videos.description: Luffy's fierce battle with Wapol to protect Dr. Hiriluk's Pirate Flag, culminating in the epic Gomu Gomu no Bazooka that sends the tyrant flying.

videos.title: Cherry Blossoms Bloom - Hiriluk's Dream Realized
videos.description: Dr. Kureha fires the cherry blossom dust into the sky, fulfilling Hiriluk's dream and healing the nation's frozen heart. The Sakura Kingdom is born.
